title: Capturing Structural Locality in Non-parametric Language Models

structured.summary: The paper proposes integrating structural locality features into kNN-based non-parametric language models to enhance retrieval relevance beyond standard contextual similarity metrics. By learning locality-conditioned distance transforms, the approach improves language modeling performance on Wikipedia text and Java source code without retraining the base model.

strengths.point: Novel integration of hierarchical locality into kNN-LMs
strengths.evidence: The method explicitly encodes multi-level structural locality features (e.g., article sections, project folders) into the retrieval distance metric via learned affine transformations, improving retrieval relevance.
Demonstrated statistically significant perplexity and accuracy improvements over both parametric LMs and vanilla kNN-LMs on WikiText-103 and Java code datasets.
The locality conditioning adds only a small number of parameters and requires no base model retraining, suggesting applicability across domains with hierarchical structure.

strengths.point: In-depth analysis of locality’s complementary role
strengths.evidence: Experiments show that structural locality captures relevance cues not explained by contextual similarity alone, supported by distance separation and retrieval accuracy analyses.

weaknesses.point: Insufficient comparison to prior locality-aware retrieval models
weaknesses.evidence: The paper lacks discussion and evaluation against existing adaptive or context-aware kNN variants and retrieval methods exploiting hierarchical or topical information.

weaknesses.point: Limited exploration of design choices for distance transformations
weaknesses.evidence: Only affine scaling and shifting per locality level are proposed without ablations or consideration of nonlinear or gating alternatives.

weaknesses.point: Unclear generalizability to datasets without explicit hierarchy
weaknesses.evidence: Locality features are manually defined from clear hierarchical signals; applicability or robustness to noisy or flat data structures is not assessed.

weaknesses.point: Differences in base LM architectures across datasets may confound results
weaknesses.evidence: Transformer depth varies between WikiText-103 (27 layers) and Java code (8 layers), complicating cross-domain performance comparisons.

weaknesses.point: Incomplete reporting of kNN retrieval hyperparameters and optimization details
weaknesses.evidence: Datastore construction size, neighbor counts, and training procedures for locality parameters are insufficiently detailed, hindering reproducibility.

weaknesses.point: Lack of latency, computational cost, and scalability analysis
weaknesses.evidence: The impact of locality-conditioned retrieval on inference speed and resource usage is not quantified.

weaknesses.point: No evaluation on downstream tasks or qualitative error analysis
weaknesses.evidence: Metrics focus on perplexity and top-k accuracy with no insights into real-world benefits, failure cases, or error modes.

weaknesses.point: Ambiguities in locality feature aggregation and parameter optimization
weaknesses.evidence: The paper does not clearly explain how multiple locality features per neighbor combine or details of the parameter optimization process.

weaknesses.point: Limited baselines excluding parametric hierarchical models
weaknesses.evidence: Comparison is mainly against vanilla Transformers and basic kNN-LMs; hierarchical or graph-based parametric models are not evaluated.

weaknesses.point: Reproducibility hindered by missing code and detailed hyperparameter configurations
weaknesses.evidence: Although reproducibility packages are mentioned, no explicit code links or comprehensive settings are provided.

questions.question: How does the method perform on datasets lacking clear hierarchical structure or with noisy locality signals?
questions.motivation: Understanding applicability to unstructured or less-organized data is critical for generalizing the approach beyond curated domains.

questions.question: Can more expressive or nonlinear transformations of locality-conditioned distances further improve retrieval quality?
questions.motivation: Exploring richer parameterizations may reveal better ways to integrate locality and potentially yield larger performance gains.

questions.question: What are the computational and latency trade-offs when applying locality-conditioned retrieval at scale?
questions.motivation: Quantifying efficiency impacts is essential for assessing practical deployment feasibility.

questions.question: How does the approach compare to parametric models explicitly modeling hierarchical or graph contexts?
questions.motivation: Evaluating against stronger baselines would clarify the relative benefits and novel contributions.

critique_points.point_001: Insufficient comparison to prior locality-aware retrieval models. The paper lacks discussion and evaluation against existing adaptive or context-aware kNN variants and retrieval methods exploiting hierarchical or topical information
critique_points.point_002: Limited exploration of design choices for distance transformations. Only affine scaling and shifting per locality level are proposed without ablations or consideration of nonlinear or gating alternatives
critique_points.point_003: Unclear generalizability to datasets without explicit hierarchy. Locality features are manually defined from clear hierarchical signals; applicability or robustness to noisy or flat data structures is not assessed
critique_points.point_004: Differences in base LM architectures across datasets may confound results. Transformer depth varies between WikiText-103 (27 layers) and Java code (8 layers), complicating cross-domain performance comparisons
critique_points.point_005: Incomplete reporting of kNN retrieval hyperparameters and optimization details. Datastore construction size, neighbor counts, and training procedures for locality parameters are insufficiently detailed, hindering reproducibility
critique_points.point_006: Lack of latency, computational cost, and scalability analysis. The impact of locality-conditioned retrieval on inference speed and resource usage is not quantified
critique_points.point_007: No evaluation on downstream tasks or qualitative error analysis. Metrics focus on perplexity and top-k accuracy with no insights into real-world benefits, failure cases, or error modes
critique_points.point_008: Ambiguities in locality feature aggregation and parameter optimization. The paper does not clearly explain how multiple locality features per neighbor combine or details of the parameter optimization process
critique_points.point_009: Limited baselines excluding parametric hierarchical models. Comparison is mainly against vanilla Transformers and basic kNN-LMs; hierarchical or graph-based parametric models are not evaluated
critique_points.point_010: Reproducibility hindered by missing code and detailed hyperparameter configurations. Although reproducibility packages are mentioned, no explicit code links or comprehensive settings are provided
